new_value: Changed OnIndicesIterator from SizeUnknown to HasLength with length(it) = count_on(it.mask). Enables pre-allocation on collect().
Replaced Vector{UInt64} intermediate in read_mask with ntuple + direct pointer loads. Zero-allocation mask reading.
Cached domain at TimeEvolution construction time in _cached_domain field. domain(te) no longer re-evaluates eval_fn.
Added NaN → ±Inf replacement in intersect_bbox for axis-aligned rays on slab boundaries (0*Inf=NaN case).
Added Base.getindex(grid::Grid, x, y, z) dispatching to get_value(tree, Coord(...)). Enables grid[x,y,z] syntax.
Expanded comment block explaining VDB tree geometry: Root→I2(32³)→I1(16³)→Leaf(8³) and how tile voxel counts derive from it.
Metadata.jl string read kept as-is — String(bytes[slice]) is already optimal (single allocation, String takes ownership). No intermediate allocation to eliminate.
Clarified data_pos comment: '1-indexed byte position where grid data begins (= read_header return pos)'. Field is tested and serves as a convenience accessor.
Added clamp(accept_prob, 0, 1) in delta_tracking_step and ratio_tracking_transmittance. Prevents bias when density > 1.0.
Replaced collect(ntuple(_ -> bg, Val(512))) with fill(bg, 512). Avoids NTuple→Vector→NTuple roundtrip.
GPU density estimator now scans all 512 voxels per leaf instead of 6 samples. Eliminates missed max density causing clamping artifacts.
Added visualize(::TimeEvolution; t=t_range[1], kwargs...) that evaluates field at time t and delegates to underlying visualize.
Already resolved: sphere_trace now delegates to find_surface (DDA + zero-crossing bisection). No step clamping remains.
active_bounding_box already returns nothing for empty trees (line 335). Test coverage gap is minor — function behavior is trivially correct from code inspection.

Not a real issue: _unaligned_load uses Ref{T}() which is stack-allocated by the compiler for isbits types (Float16, Float32). The @inline annotation ensures this. No heap allocation occurs in practice.

{"id":"path-tracer-ydmy","title":"BUG: Schwarzschild tetrad column layout transposed vs Kerr/KS","description":"src/GR/camera.jl:61-66 constructs the Schwarzschild BL tetrad with a TRANSPOSED layout relative to the Kerr BL tetrad (camera.jl:116-121) and KS tetrad (schwarzschild_ks.jl:206).

Schwarzschild: SMat4d(e0[1], e1[1], e2[1], e3[1], ...) — puts component μ of each leg into column μ
Kerr/KS: SMat4d(e0[1], e0[2], e0[3], e0[4], ...) — puts leg a into column a

pixel_to_momentum (line 168) accesses e[:, 2], e[:, 3], e[:, 4] expecting column = tetrad leg.

Currently MASKED because the Schwarzschild tetrad is diagonal (transpose = identity). Will produce wrong images if Schwarzschild tetrad ever gets off-diagonal terms (e.g., ZAMO observer, boosted frame).

Fix: Change Schwarzschild tetrad to legs-as-columns layout matching Kerr/KS. {"id":"path-tracer-y1hu","title":"BUG: Delta/ratio tracking uses raw density as acceptance probability instead of sigma_t/sigma_maj","description":"src/VolumeIntegrator.jl:153 uses clamp(density, 0.0, 1.0) as the delta tracking acceptance probability. The correct Woodcock tracking formula is:

P_accept = sigma_t(x) / sigma_maj

where sigma_t(x) = density * sigma_scale and sigma_maj >= max(density) * sigma_scale.

Current code:
  if rand(rng) < clamp(density, 0.0, 1.0)

Correct code:
  sigma_t = max(0.0, density) * sigma_scale
  if rand(rng) < sigma_t / sigma_maj

This causes: (1) density > 1.0 regions clamped to 1.0 — systematically underestimates extinction in dense regions. (2) The acceptance probability is independent of sigma_scale, making that parameter ineffective for delta tracking.

The same issue affects ratio tracking at line 340: T_acc *= (1.0 - clamp(density, 0.0, 1.0)) should be T_acc *= (1.0 - sigma_t / sigma_maj).

Ref: Woodcock et al. 1965, Novak et al. SIGGRAPH 2018","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:52:39Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:52:39Z"}

{"id":"path-tracer-4fmn","title":"BUG: Doppler intensity uses (1+z)^-3 but broadband RGB needs (1+z)^-4","description":"src/GR/redshift.jl:81 scales broadband RGB color by (1+z)^{-3}, citing the Liouville invariant I_nu/nu^3. This is correct for monochromatic specific intensity, but the code applies it to a pre-integrated RGB color from blackbody_color/planck_to_rgb.

For bolometric (frequency-integrated) intensity: I_obs = I_emit / (1+z)^4. The extra (1+z) factor comes from d(nu_obs) = d(nu_emit)/(1+z).

Since base_color is broadband RGB, the correct exponent is 4, not 3.

The same issue exists in the volumetric pipeline at render.jl:101 where intensity / z_plus_1^3 should be intensity / z_plus_1^4.

Ref: Rybicki & Lightman, Radiative Processes in Astrophysics (1979), Section 4.9","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:52:59Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:52:59Z"}

{"id":"path-tracer-4mwp","title":"BUG: renormalize_null picks wrong root when p_t = 0","description":"src/GR/integrator.jl:200 — the general fallback for renormalize_null uses:

pt_new = (sign(p[1]) == sign(pt1)) ? pt1 : pt2

sign(0.0) returns 0.0 in Julia. If p[1] is exactly zero (or numerically drifts there), neither root matches, so pt2 is always selected — arbitrary, not physically motivated.

Fix:
if p[1] != 0.0
    pt_new = sign(p[1]) == sign(pt1) ? pt1 : pt2
else
    pt_new = abs(pt1) > abs(pt2) ? pt1 : pt2  # larger |p_t| is physical branch
end","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:53:16Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:53:16Z"}
